text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic intracorporeal anastomosis, aligning the transverse colon with the descending colon at the sigmoid junction. The final stages of the resection are visible, with the transverse colon descending smoothly to meet the descending colon.

The surgeon places a stay stitch to assess the alignment for the anastomosis, then positions a stapler to evaluate the fit. Entratomies are made on the transverse colon and descending colon to facilitate the anastomosis.
The surgeon uses an L hook to create precise enterotomies in the tinea coli of the colon, preparing for the anastomosis. A mirror line is utilized to open the enterotomy.

The surgeon prepares the colon for anastomosis by ensuring the opening is sufficient for the insertion of a blue load stapler. The colon appears soft, and the stapler is positioned to utilize its full length of sixty centimeters, with the tissue coming together smoothly.
The surgeon closes the defect using a stapler after completing the resection. The video briefly disconnects during this process.

The surgeon places three sutures and performs an anastomosis using a stapler. After stapling, the surgeon reinforces the staple lines with limbered sutures, particularly where the two staple lines are close to each other on the colon.
The surgeon examines two adjacent staple lines on the bowel, ensuring they are adequately reinforced. Despite concerns about proximity, the staple lines appear intact without signs of ischemia.
